skill.name: url-analysis
skill.description: URL validation and contextual analysis specialist. Use when validating links, analyzing URL context, extracting links from content, or ensuring link appropriateness. Combines technical validation with content analysis.
skill.summary: Checks URL status, redirects, and relevance for documents and web content.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many high-risk keyword matches, but most are false positives from markdown examples and URL terminology. The confirmed risks are expected for this skill: it reads user-specified files and sends HEAD requests to extracted or provided URLs, which can expose metadata or reach internal services if run in a privileged network.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Requests to User Supplied URLs
medium_findings.description: The helper script validates arbitrary URLs with requests.head and follows redirects. This is legitimate for URL validation, but it can contact internal hosts, leak request metadata, or trigger SSRF-like behavior when run from trusted networks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network call and user-facing examples directly support the finding. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the concern is environmental risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: User Controlled File Read for Link Extraction
medium_findings.description: The helper script accepts a command-line file path and reads its contents before extracting URLs. This is useful for document audits, but users should avoid running it on sensitive files or untrusted paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path is taken from argv and read directly. There is no evidence of exfiltration, but the local file access is real.

low_findings.title: Markdown Helper Commands Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported shell execution in SKILL.md, but the cited lines are fenced examples and inline filenames. They document how a user may run the helper script and are not executed by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are markdown documentation and bash code fences. No runtime mechanism executes these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are URL and SSL Terminology
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithms, but the reviewed lines describe URL validation, HTTP status checks, SSL checks, and sample URLs. No cryptographic hashing or encryption code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed code imports no crypto library and performs no hashing. The alerts appear to match words related to status, SSL, or URLs.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Match Benign Link Analysis Language
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported system or network reconnaissance, but the cited content describes analyzing link context and checking URL accessibility. No evidence found of host scanning, port scanning, or system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wording is consistent with URL quality checks. The script makes one HTTP HEAD request per discovered URL and does not enumerate networks.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Alert Is a Static Import
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported a dynamic import expression, but scripts/validate-urls.py imports RequestException and Timeout from requests.exceptions using normal Python import syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code is a normal import statement. There is no importlib use, eval, exec, or runtime module loading.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTTP Requests Follow Redirects
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses requests.head with allow_redirects=True. This is useful for link validation but increases exposure to redirected internal or tracking endpoints.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The call and redirect behavior are explicit in the code. The risk depends on the environment where the helper runs.

content.user_title: Validate URLs and Review Link Context
content.value_statement: Broken or misleading links reduce trust in documents, websites, and support content. This skill checks URL status, redirects, context, and relevance for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Extracts HTTP and HTTPS URLs from text files.
Checks URL syntax before making network requests.
Uses HTTP HEAD requests to report status codes and redirects.
Summarizes whether links match their surrounding context.
Flags broken links, redirect chains, and low-relevance destinations.
limitations: The helper script can only validate reachable URLs from the local network.
Some sites block HEAD requests or return different results for bots.
Contextual relevance depends on the provided surrounding text.
The documentation references validate_urls.py, while the included file is named validate-urls.py.

use_cases.title: Review Documentation Links
use_cases.description: Check product docs for broken links, stale redirects, and mismatched anchor text before release.

use_cases.title: Audit Website Content
use_cases.description: Find external links that harm reader trust, SEO quality, or source relevance.

use_cases.title: Validate Support Resources
use_cases.description: Confirm that help center articles still point to accurate pages and current references.

prompt_templates.title: Check One URL
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation of a single link.
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this URL and tell me if it is reachable, secure, and appropriate for the linked text: [URL and context].

prompt_templates.title: Review a Document
prompt_templates.scenario: Scan a document for link quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract every URL from this document, check each link, and summarize broken links, redirects, and context issues.

prompt_templates.title: Audit SEO Links
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate link relevance for search quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the external links in this article for status, authority, relevance, anchor text accuracy, and SEO risk.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Link Remediation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized remediation plan for these URL validation results, grouped by broken links, redirects, security concerns, and context mismatches.

output_examples.input: Validate three links in a release note.
output: Two links are reachable and use HTTPS.
One link returns 404 and should be replaced before publication.
One redirected link should be updated to its final destination.

output_examples.input: Check whether a link matches the phrase advanced React patterns.
output_examples.output: The link is reachable, but it points to a general homepage. Use a specific article or change the link text.

output_examples.input: Audit external links in a documentation page.
output: The page contains eight external links.
Six links are appropriate for their context.
Two links need review because their destinations are outdated.

best_practices: Run checks from a network where outbound URL requests are allowed and logged.
Review redirects manually before replacing important source links.
Provide surrounding text so relevance analysis can evaluate link intent.
anti_patterns: Do not run validation against sensitive internal URLs without approval.
Do not treat HTTP status alone as proof that a source is useful.
Do not publish automated results without checking important editorial context.

faq.question: Does this skill make network requests?
faq.answer: Yes. The helper script sends HTTP HEAD requests to validate URL status and redirects.

faq.question: Can it detect broken links?
faq.answer: Yes. It reports status codes such as 404 and marks inaccessible URLs for review.

faq.question: Can it judge link relevance?
faq.answer: Yes, when the prompt includes anchor text and surrounding context.

faq.question: Does it crawl entire websites?
faq.answer: No. It validates URLs provided directly or extracted from a supplied file.

faq.question: Is it safe for private documents?
faq.answer: Use care. The script reads local files and may request URLs found in those files.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
